revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.comment: Programmatically generating so that if this gets imported to wikipedia as a draft, the bot will attribute it to Immanuelle and send them g6 warnings

revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=乎彌神社| 所在地=[[滋賀県]][[伊香郡]][[余呉町]]下余呉2053| 祭神=[[巨知人命]]・[[梨津臣命]]・[[海津見命]]| 社格=[[式内社]]
[[村社]]| 創建=[[崇神天皇]]御宇| 本殿=一間社[[流造]]| 例祭=[[4月8日]]| 神事=}} '''乎彌神社'''（おみじんじゃ）は[[滋賀県]][[伊香郡]][[余呉町]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。 ==ご祭神== * [[巨知人命]] * [[梨津臣命]] * [[海津見命]] ==ご神紋== * 左三ッ巴 ==歴史== :[[崇神天皇]]の時代に創祀されたと伝わる。[[式内社]]論社である。[[明治]]に氏子域に鎮座していた村草神社、八幡神社、大名持神社、塞神社の4社を境内に[[遷座]]した。旧村社。 ==祭事== * 例祭　　4月8日 ==境内社== * 村草神社 * 八幡神社 * 大名持神社 * 塞神社 == 関連項目 == * [[神道]] * [[神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:おみしんしや}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:村社]] [[Category:余呉町]] {{Shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=乎彌神社| 画像=[[image:Omi-jinja.JPG|center|280px]]

[[村社]]| 創建=[[崇神天皇]]御宇| 本殿=一間社[[流造]]| 例祭=[[4月8日]]| 神事=}} '''乎彌神社'''（おみじんじゃ）は[[滋賀県]][[伊香郡]][[余呉町]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。 ==ご祭神== * [[巨知人命]] * [[梨津臣命]] * [[海津見命]] ==ご神紋== * 左三ッ巴 ==歴史== :[[崇神天皇]]の時代に創祀されたと伝わる。[[式内社]]論社である。[[明治]]に氏子域に鎮座していた村草神社、八幡神社、大名持神社、塞神社の4社を境内に[[遷座]]した。旧村社。 ==祭事== * 例祭　　4月8日 ==境内社== * 村草神社 * 八幡神社 * 大名持神社 * 塞神社 == 関連項目 == * [[神道]] * [[神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:おみしんしや}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:村社]] [[Category:余呉町]] {{Shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 乎彌神社 |画像 = [[image:Omi-jinja.JPG|center|250px]]
乎彌神社 正面 |所在地 = [[滋賀県]][[長浜市]]余呉町下余呉2053 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|31|56.5|N|136|12|16.3|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 巨知人命
海津見命
梨津臣命 |社格 = 式内小社論社・旧村社 |創建 = 伝崇神天皇朝 |本殿 = [[流造|一間社流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[4月8日]] |神事 = }} '''乎彌神社'''（おみじんじゃ）は[[滋賀県]][[長浜市]][[余呉町]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。 == 祭神 == * [[巨知人命|巨知人命（おみしるひとのみこと）]] * [[綿津見神|海津見命]] * [[梨津臣命|梨津臣命（なしとみのみこと）]]（乃弥神社） == 神紋 == * 左三ッ巴 == 歴史 == [[崇神天皇]]の時代に創祀されたと伝わる。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』[[伊香郡|近江国伊香郡]]の小社「乎弥神社」に比定され（[[式内社論社]]）他の論社として同じ長浜市内の[[大見神社]]（[[木之本町]]）と、[[春日神社 (長浜市高月町)|春日神社]]（[[高月町]]）がある。、同じく式内小社の「乃弥神社」を[[合祀]]している。 [[明治]]に[[氏子]]域に鎮座していた村草神社、八幡神社、大名持神社、塞神社の4社を境内に[[遷座]]した。[[旧村社]]。 == 祭事 == * 例祭 - 4月8日 == 境内社 == * 村草神社 * 八幡神社 * 大名持神社 * 塞神社 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1267 乎彌神社]（滋賀県神社庁） {{DEFAULTSORT:おみしんしや}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:村社]] [[Category:長浜市]] {{Shinto-stub}}

海津見命
梨津臣命 |社格 = 式内小社論社・旧村社 |創建 = 伝崇神天皇朝 |本殿 = [[流造|一間社流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[4月8日]] |神事 = }} '''乎彌神社'''（おみじんじゃ）は[[滋賀県]][[長浜市]][[余呉町]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。 == 祭神 == * [[巨知人命|巨知人命（おみしるひとのみこと）]] * [[綿津見神|海津見命]] * [[梨津臣命|梨津臣命（なしとみのみこと）]]（乃弥神社） == 神紋 == * 左三ッ巴 == 歴史 == [[崇神天皇]]の時代に創祀されたと伝わる。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』[[伊香郡|近江国伊香郡]]の小社「乎弥神社」に比定され（[[式内社論社]]）他の論社として同じ長浜市内の[[大見神社]]（[[木之本町]]）と、[[春日神社 (長浜市高月町)|春日神社]]（[[高月町]]）がある。、同じく式内小社の「乃弥神社」を[[合祀]]している。 [[明治]]に[[氏子]]域に鎮座していた村草神社、八幡神社、大名持神社、塞神社の4社を境内に[[遷座]]した。[[旧村社]]。 == 祭事 == * 例祭 - 4月8日 == 境内社 == * 村草神社 * 八幡神社 * 大名持神社 * 塞神社 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1267 乎彌神社]（滋賀県神社庁） {{DEFAULTSORT:おみしんしや}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:長浜市]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 乎彌神社 |画像 = [[image:Omi-jinja.JPG|center|250px]]
乎彌神社 正面 |所在地 = [[滋賀県]][[長浜市]]余呉町下余呉2053 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|31|56.5|N|136|12|16.3|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 巨知人命
海津見命
梨津臣命 |社格 = 式内小社論社・旧村社 |創建 = 伝崇神天皇朝 |本殿 = [[流造|一間社流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[4月8日]] |神事 = }} '''乎彌神社'''（おみじんじゃ）は[[滋賀県]][[長浜市]]余呉町下余呉に鎮座する[[神社]]である。 == 祭神 == * [[巨知人命]]（おみしるひとのみこと） * [[綿津見神|海津見命]] * [[梨津臣命]]（なしとみのみこと）（乃弥神社） == 神紋 == * 左三ッ巴 == 歴史 == [[崇神天皇]]の時代に創祀されたと伝わる。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』[[伊香郡|近江国伊香郡]]の小社「乎弥神社」に比定され（[[式内社論社]]）他の論社として同じ長浜市内の[[大見神社]]（木之本町大見）と、[[春日神社 (長浜市高月町宇根)|春日神社]]（高月町宇根）がある。、同じく式内小社の「乃弥神社」を[[合祀]]している。 [[明治]]に[[氏子]]域に鎮座していた村草神社、八幡神社、大名持神社、塞神社の4社を境内に[[遷座]]した。[[旧村社]]。 == 祭事 == * 例祭 - 4月8日 == 境内社 == * 村草神社 * 八幡神社 * 大名持神社 * 塞神社 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1267 乎彌神社]（滋賀県神社庁） {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おみしんしや}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:長浜市の歴史]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Omi Shrine | Image = [[image:Omi-jinja.JPG|center|250px]]
Front view of Omi Shrine | Location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shimoyogo 2053 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|56.5|N|136|12|16.3|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}}
{{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}}
{{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legend) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (single-bay structure) | Alternate_names = | Notable_shrines = | Festival = {{ill|April 8|ja|4月8日}} | Rituals = }} '''Omi Shrine''' (乎彌神社, Omi Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. == Deities == * {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}} (Sea God) * {{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} (worshipped at {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}) == Shrine crest == * Left Mitsudomoe (三つ巴) == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. It is identified as the small shrine "Omi Jinja" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Ika District|ja|伊香郡}}, {{ill|Omi Province|ja|近江国}}, and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (disputed location).Other possible locations for the shrine include {{ill|Omi Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|大見神社}} in Kinomoto Town, Nagahama, and {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|春日神社 (長浜市高月町宇根)}} in Takatsuki Town, Nagahama. The shrine also enshrines the deities of the {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}, another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. During the [[Meiji period]], four shrines within the shrine parish—Murakusa Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Daimyojin Shrine, and Seki Shrine—were relocated to the shrine grounds. It was designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Festivals == * Main Festival: April 8 == Auxiliary shrines == * {{ill|Murakusa Shrine|ja|村草神社}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} * {{ill|Daimyojin Shrine|ja|大名持神社}} * {{ill|Seki Shrine|ja|塞神社}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1267 Omi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{ja icon}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:History of Nagahama]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagahama]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Omi Province]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Former village shrines of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[ja:乎彌神社]]

It was designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Festivals == * Main Festival: April 8 == Auxiliary shrines == * {{ill|Murakusa Shrine|ja|村草神社}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} * {{ill|Daimyojin Shrine|ja|大名持神社}} * {{ill|Seki Shrine|ja|塞神社}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1267 Omi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{ja icon}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:History of Nagahama]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagahama]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Omi Province]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Former village shrines of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[ja:乎彌神社]]

Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legend) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (single-bay structure) | Alternate_names = | Notable_shrines = | Festival = {{ill|April 8|ja|4月8日}} | Rituals = }} '''Omi Shrine''' (乎彌神社, Omi Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. == Deities == * {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}} (Sea God) * {{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} (worshipped at {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}) == Shrine crest == * Left Mitsudomoe (三つ巴) == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. It is identified as the small shrine "Omi Jinja" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Ika District|ja|伊香郡}}, {{ill|Omi Province|ja|近江国}}, and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (disputed location).Other possible locations for the shrine include {{ill|Omi Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|大見神社}} in Kinomoto Town, Nagahama, and {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|春日神社 (長浜市高月町宇根)}} in Takatsuki Town, Nagahama. The shrine also enshrines the deities of the {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}, another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. During the [[Meiji period]], four shrines within the shrine parish—Murakusa Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Daimyojin Shrine, and Seki Shrine—were relocated to the shrine grounds. It was designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Festivals == * Main Festival: April 8 == Auxiliary shrines == * {{ill|Murakusa Shrine|ja|村草神社}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} * {{ill|Daimyojin Shrine|ja|大名持神社}} * {{ill|Seki Shrine|ja|塞神社}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1267 Omi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{ja icon}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:History of Nagahama]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagahama]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Omi Province]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Former village shrines of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[ja:乎彌神社]]

It is identified as the small shrine "Omi Jinja" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Ika District|ja|伊香郡}}, {{ill|Omi Province|ja|近江国}}, and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (disputed location).Other possible locations for the shrine include {{ill|Omi Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|大見神社}} in Kinomoto Town, Nagahama, and {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|春日神社 (長浜市高月町宇根)}} in Takatsuki Town, Nagahama. The shrine also enshrines the deities of the {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}, another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. During the [[Meiji period]], four shrines within the shrine parish—Murakusa Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Daimyojin Shrine, and Seki Shrine—were relocated to the shrine grounds. It was designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Festivals == * Main Festival: April 8 == Auxiliary shrines == * {{ill|Murakusa Shrine|ja|村草神社}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} * {{ill|Daimyojin Shrine|ja|大名持神社}} * {{ill|Seki Shrine|ja|塞神社}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1267 Omi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{ja icon}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Nagahama]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagahama]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Omi Province]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Former village shrines of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:乎彌神社]]

{{Shrine | name = Omi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:Omi-jinja.JPG|center|250px]]
Front view of Omi Shrine | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shimoyogo 2053 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|56.5|N|136|12|16.3|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}}
{{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}}
{{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legend) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (single-bay structure) | Alternate_names = | Notable_shrines = | Festival = {{ill|April 8|ja|4月8日}} | Rituals = }} '''Omi Shrine''' (乎彌神社, Omi Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. == Deities == * {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}} (Sea God) * {{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} (worshipped at {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}) == Shrine crest == * Left Mitsudomoe (三つ巴) == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. It is identified as the small shrine "Omi Jinja" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Ika District|ja|伊香郡}}, {{ill|Omi Province|ja|近江国}}, and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (disputed location).Other possible locations for the shrine include {{ill|Omi Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|大見神社}} in Kinomoto Town, Nagahama, and {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|春日神社 (長浜市高月町宇根)}} in Takatsuki Town, Nagahama. The shrine also enshrines the deities of the {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}, another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. During the [[Meiji period]], four shrines within the shrine parish—Murakusa Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Daimyojin Shrine, and Seki Shrine—were relocated to the shrine grounds. It was designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Festivals == * Main Festival: April 8 == Auxiliary shrines == * {{ill|Murakusa Shrine|ja|村草神社}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} * {{ill|Daimyojin Shrine|ja|大名持神社}} * {{ill|Seki Shrine|ja|塞神社}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1267 Omi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{ja icon}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Nagahama]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagahama]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Omi Province]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Former village shrines of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:乎彌神社]]

It is identified as the small shrine "Omi Jinja" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Ika District|ja|伊香郡}}, {{ill|Omi Province|ja|近江国}}, and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (disputed location).Other possible locations for the shrine include {{ill|Omi Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|大見神社}} in Kinomoto Town, Nagahama, and {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|春日神社 (長浜市高月町宇根)}} in Takatsuki Town, Nagahama. During the [[Meiji period]], four shrines within the shrine parish—Murakusa Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Daimyojin Shrine, and Seki Shrine—were relocated to the shrine grounds. It was designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Festivals == * Main Festival: April 8 == Auxiliary shrines == * {{ill|Murakusa Shrine|ja|村草神社}} * revision.comment: fixing category error
revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Omi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:Omi-jinja.JPG|center|250px]]
Front view of Omi Shrine | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shimoyogo 2053 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|56.5|N|136|12|16.3|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}}
{{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}}
{{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legend) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (single-bay structure) | Alternate_names = | Notable_shrines = | Festival = {{ill|April 8|ja|4月8日}} | Rituals = }} '''Omi Shrine''' (乎彌神社, Omi Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. == Deities == * {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}} (Sea God) * {{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} (worshipped at {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}) == Shrine crest == * Left Mitsudomoe (三つ巴) == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. It is identified as the small shrine "Omi Jinja" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Ika District|ja|伊香郡}}, {{ill|Omi Province|ja|近江国}}, and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (disputed location).Other possible locations for the shrine include {{ill|Omi Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|大見神社}} in Kinomoto Town, Nagahama, and {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|春日神社 (長浜市高月町宇根)}} in Takatsuki Town, Nagahama. The shrine also enshrines the deities of the {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}, another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. During the [[Meiji period]], four shrines within the shrine parish—Murakusa Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Daimyojin Shrine, and Seki Shrine—were relocated to the shrine grounds. It was designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Festivals == * Main Festival: April 8 == Auxiliary shrines == * {{ill|Murakusa Shrine|ja|村草神社}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} * {{ill|Daimyojin Shrine|ja|大名持神社}} * {{ill|Seki Shrine|ja|塞神社}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1267 Omi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{ja icon}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shrine | name = Omi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:Omi-jinja.JPG|center|250px]]
Front view of Omi Shrine | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shimoyogo 2053 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|56.5|N|136|12|16.3|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}}
{{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}}
{{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legend) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (single-bay structure) | Alternate_names = | Notable_shrines = | Festival = {{ill|April 8|ja|4月8日}} | Rituals = }} '''Omi Shrine''' (乎彌神社, Omi Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. == Deities == * {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}} (Sea God) * {{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} (worshipped at {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}) == Shrine crest == * Left Mitsudomoe (三つ巴) == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. It is identified as the small shrine "Omi Jinja" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Ika District|ja|伊香郡}}, {{ill|Omi Province|ja|近江国}}, and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (disputed location).Other possible locations for the shrine include {{ill|Omi Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|大見神社}} in Kinomoto Town, Nagahama, and {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|春日神社 (長浜市高月町宇根)}} in Takatsuki Town, Nagahama. The shrine also enshrines the deities of the {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}, another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. During the [[Meiji period]], four shrines within the shrine parish—Murakusa Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Daimyojin Shrine, and Seki Shrine—were relocated to the shrine grounds. It was designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Festivals == * Main Festival: April 8 == Auxiliary shrines == * {{ill|Murakusa Shrine|ja|村草神社}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} * {{ill|Daimyojin Shrine|ja|大名持神社}} * {{ill|Seki Shrine|ja|塞神社}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1267 Omi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{ja icon}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shrine | name = Omi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:Omi-jinja.JPG|center|250px]]
Front view of Omi Shrine | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shimoyogo 2053 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|56.5|N|136|12|16.3|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}}
{{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}}
{{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legend) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (single-bay structure) | Alternate_names = | Notable_shrines = | Festival = {{ill|April 8|ja|4月8日}} | Rituals = }} '''Omi Shrine''' (乎彌神社, Omi Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. == Deities == * {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}} (Sea God) * {{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} (worshipped at {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}) == Shrine crest == * Left Mitsudomoe (三つ巴) == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. It is identified as the small shrine "Omi Jinja" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Ika District|ja|伊香郡}}, {{ill|Omi Province|ja|近江国}}, and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (disputed location).Other possible locations for the shrine include {{ill|Omi Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|大見神社}} in Kinomoto Town, Nagahama, and {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|春日神社 (長浜市高月町宇根)}} in Takatsuki Town, Nagahama. The shrine also enshrines the deities of the {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}, another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. During the [[Meiji period]], four shrines within the shrine parish—Murakusa Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Daimyojin Shrine, and Seki Shrine—were relocated to the shrine grounds. It was designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Festivals == * Main Festival: April 8 == Auxiliary shrines == * {{ill|Murakusa Shrine|ja|村草神社}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} * {{ill|Daimyojin Shrine|ja|大名持神社}} * {{ill|Seki Shrine|ja|塞神社}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == * {{Shrine | name = Omi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:Omi-jinja.JPG|center|250px]]
Front view of Omi Shrine | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shimoyogo 2053 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|56.5|N|136|12|16.3|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}}
{{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}}
{{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legend) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (single-bay structure) | Alternate_names = | Notable_shrines = | Festival = {{ill|April 8|ja|4月8日}} | Rituals = }} '''Omi Shrine''' (乎彌神社, Omi Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. == Deities == * {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}} (Sea God) * {{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} (worshipped at {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}) == Shrine crest == * Left Mitsudomoe (三つ巴) == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. It is identified as the small shrine "Omi Jinja" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Ika District|ja|伊香郡}}, {{ill|Omi Province|ja|近江国}}, and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (disputed location).Other possible locations for the shrine include {{ill|Omi Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|大見神社}} in Kinomoto Town, Nagahama, and {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|春日神社 (長浜市高月町宇根)}} in Takatsuki Town, Nagahama. The shrine also enshrines the deities of the {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}, another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. During the [[Meiji period]], four shrines within the shrine parish—Murakusa Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Daimyojin Shrine, and Seki Shrine—were relocated to the shrine grounds. It was designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Festivals == * Main Festival: April 8 == Auxiliary shrines == * {{ill|Murakusa Shrine|ja|村草神社}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} * {{ill|Daimyojin Shrine|ja|大名持神社}} * {{ill|Seki Shrine|ja|塞神社}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == * It is identified as the small shrine "Omi Jinja" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Ika District|ja|伊香郡}}, {{ill|Omi Province|ja|近江国}}, and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (disputed location).Other possible locations for the shrine include {{ill|Omi Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|大見神社}} in Kinomoto Town, Nagahama, and {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|春日神社 (長浜市高月町宇根)}} in Takatsuki Town, Nagahama. The shrine also enshrines the deities of the {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}, another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. During the [[Meiji period]], four shrines within the shrine parish—Murakusa Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Daimyojin Shrine, and Seki Shrine—were relocated to the shrine grounds. It was designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Festivals == * Main Festival: April 8 == Auxiliary shrines == * {{ill|Murakusa Shrine|ja|村草神社}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} * {{ill|Daimyojin Shrine|ja|大名持神社}} * {{ill|Seki Shrine|ja|塞神社}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == * {{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legend) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (single-bay structure) | Alternate_names = | Notable_shrines = | Festival = {{ill|April 8|ja|4月8日}} | Rituals = }} '''Omi Shrine''' (乎彌神社, Omi Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. == Deities == * {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}} (Sea God) * {{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} (worshipped at {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}) == Shrine crest == * Left Mitsudomoe (三つ巴) == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. It is identified as the small shrine "Omi Jinja" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Ika District|ja|伊香郡}}, {{ill|Omi Province|ja|近江国}}, and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (disputed location).Other possible locations for the shrine include {{ill|Omi Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|大見神社}} in Kinomoto Town, Nagahama, and {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|春日神社 (長浜市高月町宇根)}} in Takatsuki Town, Nagahama. The shrine also enshrines the deities of the {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}, another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. During the [[Meiji period]], four shrines within the shrine parish—Murakusa Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Daimyojin Shrine, and Seki Shrine—were relocated to the shrine grounds. It was designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Festivals == * Main Festival: April 8 == Auxiliary shrines == * {{ill|Murakusa Shrine|ja|村草神社}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} * {{ill|Daimyojin Shrine|ja|大名持神社}} * {{ill|Seki Shrine|ja|塞神社}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == * Front view of Omi Shrine | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shimoyogo 2053 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|56.5|N|136|12|16.3|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}}
{{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}}
{{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legend) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (single-bay structure) | Alternate_names = | Notable_shrines = | Festival = {{ill|April 8|ja|4月8日}} | Rituals = }} '''Omi Shrine''' (乎彌神社, Omi Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. == Deities == * {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}} (Sea God) * {{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} (worshipped at {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}) == Shrine crest == * Left Mitsudomoe (三つ巴) == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. It is identified as the small shrine "Omi Jinja" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Ika District|ja|伊香郡}}, {{ill|Omi Province|ja|近江国}}, and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (disputed location).Other possible locations for the shrine include {{ill|Omi Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|大見神社}} in Kinomoto Town, Nagahama, and {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|春日神社 (長浜市高月町宇根)}} in Takatsuki Town, Nagahama. The shrine also enshrines the deities of the {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}, another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. During the [[Meiji period]], four shrines within the shrine parish—Murakusa Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Daimyojin Shrine, and Seki Shrine—were relocated to the shrine grounds. It was designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Festivals == * Main Festival: April 8 == Auxiliary shrines == * {{ill|Murakusa Shrine|ja|村草神社}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} * {{ill|Daimyojin Shrine|ja|大名持神社}} * {{ill|Seki Shrine|ja|塞神社}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1267 Omi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{ja icon}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legend) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (single-bay structure) | Alternate_names = | Notable_shrines = | Festival = {{ill|April 8|ja|4月8日}} | Rituals = }} '''Omi Shrine''' (乎彌神社, Omi Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. == Deities == * {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}} (Sea God) * {{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} (worshipped at {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}) == Shrine crest == * Left Mitsudomoe (三つ巴) == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. It is identified as the small shrine "Omi Jinja" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Ika District|ja|伊香郡}}, {{ill|Omi Province|ja|近江国}}, and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (disputed location).Other possible locations for the shrine include {{ill|Omi Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|大見神社}} in Kinomoto Town, Nagahama, and {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|春日神社 (長浜市高月町宇根)}} in Takatsuki Town, Nagahama. The shrine also enshrines the deities of the {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}, another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. During the [[Meiji period]], four shrines within the shrine parish—Murakusa Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Daimyojin Shrine, and Seki Shrine—were relocated to the shrine grounds. It was designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Festivals == * Main Festival: April 8 == Auxiliary shrines == * {{ill|Murakusa Shrine|ja|村草神社}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} * {{ill|Daimyojin Shrine|ja|大名持神社}} * {{ill|Seki Shrine|ja|塞神社}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == * Front view of Omi Shrine | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shimoyogo 2053 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|56.5|N|136|12|16.3|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}}
{{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}}
{{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legend) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (single-bay structure) | Alternate_names = | Notable_shrines = | Festival = {{ill|April 8|ja|4月8日}} | Rituals = }} '''Omi Shrine''' (乎彌神社, Omi Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. == Deities == * {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}} (Sea God) * {{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} (worshipped at {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}) == Shrine crest == * Left Mitsudomoe (三つ巴) == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. It is identified as the small shrine "Omi Jinja" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Ika District|ja|伊香郡}}, {{ill|Omi Province|ja|近江国}}, and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (disputed location).Other possible locations for the shrine include {{ill|Omi Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|大見神社}} in Kinomoto Town, Nagahama, and {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|春日神社 (長浜市高月町宇根)}} in Takatsuki Town, Nagahama. The shrine also enshrines the deities of the {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}, another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. During the [[Meiji period]], four shrines within the shrine parish—Murakusa Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Daimyojin Shrine, and Seki Shrine—were relocated to the shrine grounds. It was designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Festivals == * Main Festival: April 8 == Auxiliary shrines == * {{ill|Murakusa Shrine|ja|村草神社}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} * {{ill|Daimyojin Shrine|ja|大名持神社}} * {{ill|Seki Shrine|ja|塞神社}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1267 Omi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{ja icon}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Omi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:Omi-jinja.JPG|center|250px]]
Front view of Omi Shrine | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shimoyogo 2053 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|56.5|N|136|12|16.3|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}}
{{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}}
{{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legend) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (single-bay structure) | Alternate_names = | Notable_shrines = | Festival = {{ill|April 8|ja|4月8日}} | Rituals = }} '''Omi Shrine''' (乎彌神社, Omi Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. == Deities == * {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}} (Sea God) * {{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} (worshipped at {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}) == Shrine crest == * Left Mitsudomoe (三つ巴) == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. It is identified as the small shrine "Omi Jinja" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Ika District|ja|伊香郡}}, {{ill|Omi Province|ja|近江国}}, and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (disputed location).Other possible locations for the shrine include {{ill|Omi Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|大見神社}} in Kinomoto Town, Nagahama, and {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|春日神社 (長浜市高月町宇根)}} in Takatsuki Town, Nagahama. The shrine also enshrines the deities of the {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}, another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. During the [[Meiji period]], four shrines within the shrine parish—Murakusa Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Daimyojin Shrine, and Seki Shrine—were relocated to the shrine grounds. It was designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Festivals == * Main Festival: April 8 == Auxiliary shrines == * {{ill|Murakusa Shrine|ja|村草神社}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} * {{ill|Daimyojin Shrine|ja|大名持神社}} * {{ill|Seki Shrine|ja|塞神社}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1267 Omi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{ja icon}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Omi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:Omi-jinja.JPG|center|250px]]
Front view of Omi Shrine | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shimoyogo 2053 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|56.5|N|136|12|16.3|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}}
{{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}}
{{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legend) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (single-bay structure) | Alternate_names = | Notable_shrines = | Festival = {{ill|April 8|ja|4月8日}} | Rituals = }} '''Omi Shrine''' (乎彌神社, Omi Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. == Deities == * {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}} (Sea God) * {{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} (worshipped at {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}) == Shrine crest == * Left Mitsudomoe (三つ巴) == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. It is identified as the small shrine "Omi Jinja" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Ika District|ja|伊香郡}}, {{ill|Omi Province|ja|近江国}}, and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (disputed location).Other possible locations for the shrine include {{ill|Omi Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|大見神社}} in Kinomoto Town, Nagahama, and {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|春日神社 (長浜市高月町宇根)}} in Takatsuki Town, Nagahama. The shrine also enshrines the deities of the {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}, another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. During the [[Meiji period]], four shrines within the shrine parish—Murakusa Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Daimyojin Shrine, and Seki Shrine—were relocated to the shrine grounds. It was designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Festivals == * Main Festival: April 8 == Auxiliary shrines == * {{ill|Murakusa Shrine|ja|村草神社}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} * {{ill|Daimyojin Shrine|ja|大名持神社}} * {{ill|Seki Shrine|ja|塞神社}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1267 Omi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{ja icon}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Omi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:Omi-jinja.JPG|center|250px]]
Front view of Omi Shrine | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shimoyogo 2053 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|56.5|N|136|12|16.3|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}}
{{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}}
{{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legend) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (single-bay structure) | Alternate_names = | Notable_shrines = | Festival = {{ill|April 8|ja|4月8日}} | Rituals = }} '''Omi Shrine''' (乎彌神社, Omi Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. == Deities == * {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}} (Sea God) * {{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} (worshipped at {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}) == Shrine crest == * Left Mitsudomoe (三つ巴) == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. It is identified as the small shrine "Omi Jinja" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Ika District|ja|伊香郡}}, {{ill|Omi Province|ja|近江国}}, and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (disputed location).Other possible locations for the shrine include {{ill|Omi Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|大見神社}} in Kinomoto Town, Nagahama, and {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|春日神社 (長浜市高月町宇根)}} in Takatsuki Town, Nagahama. The shrine also enshrines the deities of the {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}, another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. During the [[Meiji period]], four shrines within the shrine parish—Murakusa Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Daimyojin Shrine, and Seki Shrine—were relocated to the shrine grounds. It was designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Festivals == * Main Festival: April 8 == Auxiliary shrines == * {{ill|Murakusa Shrine|ja|村草神社}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} * {{ill|Daimyojin Shrine|ja|大名持神社}} * {{ill|Seki Shrine|ja|塞神社}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Omi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:Omi-jinja.JPG|center|250px]]
Front view of Omi Shrine | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shimoyogo 2053 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|56.5|N|136|12|16.3|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}}
{{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}}
{{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legend) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (single-bay structure) | Alternate_names = | Notable_shrines = | Festival = {{ill|April 8|ja|4月8日}} | Rituals = }} '''Omi Shrine''' (乎彌神社, Omi Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. == Deities == * {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}} (Sea God) * {{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} (worshipped at {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}) == Shrine crest == * Left Mitsudomoe (三つ巴) == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. It is identified as the small shrine "Omi Jinja" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Ika District|ja|伊香郡}}, {{ill|Omi Province|ja|近江国}}, and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (disputed location).Other possible locations for the shrine include {{ill|Omi Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|大見神社}} in Kinomoto Town, Nagahama, and {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|春日神社 (長浜市高月町宇根)}} in Takatsuki Town, Nagahama. The shrine also enshrines the deities of the {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}, another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. During the [[Meiji period]], four shrines within the shrine parish—Murakusa Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Daimyojin Shrine, and Seki Shrine—were relocated to the shrine grounds. It was designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Festivals == * Main Festival: April 8 == Auxiliary shrines == * {{ill|Murakusa Shrine|ja|村草神社}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} * {{ill|Daimyojin Shrine|ja|大名持神社}} * {{ill|Seki Shrine|ja|塞神社}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1267 Omi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{ja icon}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Omi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:Omi-jinja.JPG|center|250px]]
Front view of Omi Shrine | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shimoyogo 2053 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|56.5|N|136|12|16.3|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}}
{{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}}
{{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legend) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (single-bay structure) | Alternate_names = | Notable_shrines = | Festival = {{ill|April 8|ja|4月8日}} | Rituals = }} '''Omi Shrine''' (乎彌神社, Omi Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. == Deities == * {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}} (Sea God) * {{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} (worshipped at {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}) == Shrine crest == * Left Mitsudomoe (三つ巴) == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. It is identified as the small shrine "Omi Jinja" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Ika District|ja|伊香郡}}, {{ill|Omi Province|ja|近江国}}, and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (disputed location).Other possible locations for the shrine include {{ill|Omi Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|大見神社}} in Kinomoto Town, Nagahama, and {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|春日神社 (長浜市高月町宇根)}} in Takatsuki Town, Nagahama. The shrine also enshrines the deities of the {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}, another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. During the [[Meiji period]], four shrines within the shrine parish—Murakusa Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Daimyojin Shrine, and Seki Shrine—were relocated to the shrine grounds. It was designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Festivals == * Main Festival: April 8 == Auxiliary shrines == * {{ill|Murakusa Shrine|ja|村草神社}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} * {{ill|Daimyojin Shrine|ja|大名持神社}} * {{ill|Seki Shrine|ja|塞神社}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1267 Omi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{ja icon}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legend) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (single-bay structure) | Alternate_names = | Notable_shrines = | Festival = {{ill|April 8|ja|4月8日}} | Rituals = }} '''Omi Shrine''' (乎彌神社, Omi Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. == Deities == * {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}} (Sea God) * {{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} (worshipped at {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}) == Shrine crest == * Left Mitsudomoe (三つ巴) == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. It is identified as the small shrine "Omi Jinja" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Ika District|ja|伊香郡}}, {{ill|Omi Province|ja|近江国}}, and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (disputed location).Other possible locations for the shrine include {{ill|Omi Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|大見神社}} in Kinomoto Town, Nagahama, and {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|春日神社 (長浜市高月町宇根)}} in Takatsuki Town, Nagahama. The shrine also enshrines the deities of the {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}, another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. During the [[Meiji period]], four shrines within the shrine parish—Murakusa Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Daimyojin Shrine, and Seki Shrine—were relocated to the shrine grounds. It was designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Festivals == * Main Festival: April 8 == Auxiliary shrines == * {{ill|Murakusa Shrine|ja|村草神社}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} * {{ill|Daimyojin Shrine|ja|大名持神社}} * {{ill|Seki Shrine|ja|塞神社}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == * revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Omi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:Omi-jinja.JPG|center|250px]]
Front view of Omi Shrine | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shimoyogo 2053 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|56.5|N|136|12|16.3|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}}
{{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}}
{{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legend) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (single-bay structure) | Alternate_names = | Notable_shrines = | Festival = {{ill|April 8|ja|4月8日}} | Rituals = }} '''Omi Shrine''' (乎彌神社, Omi Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. == Deities == * {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}} (Sea God) * {{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} (worshipped at {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}) == Shrine crest == * Left Mitsudomoe (三つ巴) == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. It is identified as the small shrine "Omi Jinja" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Ika District|ja|伊香郡}}, {{ill|Omi Province|ja|近江国}}, and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (disputed location).Other possible locations for the shrine include {{ill|Omi Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|大見神社}} in Kinomoto Town, Nagahama, and {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|春日神社 (長浜市高月町宇根)}} in Takatsuki Town, Nagahama. The shrine also enshrines the deities of the {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}, another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. During the [[Meiji period]], four shrines within the shrine parish—Murakusa Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Daimyojin Shrine, and Seki Shrine—were relocated to the shrine grounds. It was designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Festivals == * Main Festival: April 8 == Auxiliary shrines == * {{ill|Murakusa Shrine|ja|村草神社}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} * {{ill|Daimyojin Shrine|ja|大名持神社}} * {{ill|Seki Shrine|ja|塞神社}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1267 Omi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{ja icon}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) (disputed location)

It is identified as the small shrine "Omi Jinja" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Ika District|ja|伊香郡}}, {{ill|Omi Province|ja|近江国}}, and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (disputed location).Other possible locations for the shrine include {{ill|Omi Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|大見神社}} in Kinomoto Town, Nagahama, and {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|春日神社 (長浜市高月町宇根)}} in Takatsuki Town, Nagahama. The shrine also enshrines the deities of the {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}, another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. During the [[Meiji period]], four shrines within the shrine parish—Murakusa Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Daimyojin Shrine, and Seki Shrine—were relocated to the shrine grounds. It was designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Festivals == * Main Festival: April 8 == Auxiliary shrines == * {{ill|Murakusa Shrine|ja|村草神社}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} * {{ill|Daimyojin Shrine|ja|大名持神社}} * {{ill|Seki Shrine|ja|塞神社}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Omi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:Omi-jinja.JPG|center|250px]]
Front view of Omi Shrine | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shimoyogo 2053 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|56.5|N|136|12|16.3|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}}
{{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}}
{{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legend) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (single-bay structure) | Alternate_names = | Notable_shrines = | Festival = {{ill|April 8|ja|4月8日}} | Rituals = }} '''Omi Shrine''' (乎彌神社, Omi Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. == Deities == * {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}} (Sea God) * {{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} (worshipped at {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}) == Shrine crest == * Left Mitsudomoe (三つ巴) == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. It is identified as the small shrine "Omi Jinja" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Ika District|ja|伊香郡}}, {{ill|Omi Province|ja|近江国}}, and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (disputed location).Other possible locations for the shrine include {{ill|Omi Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|大見神社}} in Kinomoto Town, Nagahama, and {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|春日神社 (長浜市高月町宇根)}} in Takatsuki Town, Nagahama. The shrine also enshrines the deities of the {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}, another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. During the [[Meiji period]], four shrines within the shrine parish—Murakusa Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Daimyojin Shrine, and Seki Shrine—were relocated to the shrine grounds. It was designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Festivals == * Main Festival: April 8 == Auxiliary shrines == * {{ill|Murakusa Shrine|ja|村草神社}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} * {{ill|Daimyojin Shrine|ja|大名持神社}} * {{ill|Seki Shrine|ja|塞神社}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1267 Omi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{ja icon}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Omi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:Omi-jinja.JPG|center|250px]]
Front view of Omi Shrine | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shimoyogo 2053 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|56.5|N|136|12|16.3|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}}
{{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}}
{{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legend) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (single-bay structure) | Alternate_names = | Notable_shrines = | Festival = {{ill|April 8|ja|4月8日}} | Rituals = }} '''Omi Shrine''' (乎彌神社, Omi Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. == Deities == * {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}} (Sea God) * {{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} (worshipped at {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}) == Shrine crest == * Left Mitsudomoe (三つ巴) == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. It is identified as the small shrine "Omi Jinja" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Ika District|ja|伊香郡}}, {{ill|Omi Province|ja|近江国}}, and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (disputed location).Other possible locations for the shrine include {{ill|Omi Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|大見神社}} in Kinomoto Town, Nagahama, and {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|春日神社 (長浜市高月町宇根)}} in Takatsuki Town, Nagahama. The shrine also enshrines the deities of the {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}, another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. During the [[Meiji period]], four shrines within the shrine parish—Murakusa Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Daimyojin Shrine, and Seki Shrine—were relocated to the shrine grounds. It was designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Festivals == * Main Festival: April 8 == Auxiliary shrines == * {{ill|Murakusa Shrine|ja|村草神社}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} * {{ill|Daimyojin Shrine|ja|大名持神社}} * {{ill|Seki Shrine|ja|塞神社}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1267 Omi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{ja icon}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legend) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (single-bay structure) | Alternate_names = | Notable_shrines = | Festival = {{ill|April 8|ja|4月8日}} | Rituals = }} '''Omi Shrine''' (乎彌神社, Omi Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. == Deities == * {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}} (Sea God) * {{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} (worshipped at {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}) == Shrine crest == * Left Mitsudomoe (三つ巴) == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. It is identified as the small shrine "Omi Jinja" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Ika District|ja|伊香郡}}, {{ill|Omi Province|ja|近江国}}, and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (disputed location).Other possible locations for the shrine include {{ill|Omi Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|大見神社}} in Kinomoto Town, Nagahama, and {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|春日神社 (長浜市高月町宇根)}} in Takatsuki Town, Nagahama. The shrine also enshrines the deities of the {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}, another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. During the [[Meiji period]], four shrines within the shrine parish—Murakusa Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Daimyojin Shrine, and Seki Shrine—were relocated to the shrine grounds. It was designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Festivals == * Main Festival: April 8 == Auxiliary shrines == * {{ill|Murakusa Shrine|ja|村草神社}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} * {{ill|Daimyojin Shrine|ja|大名持神社}} * {{ill|Seki Shrine|ja|塞神社}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Omi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:Omi-jinja.JPG|center|250px]]
Front view of Omi Shrine | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shimoyogo 2053 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|56.5|N|136|12|16.3|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}}
{{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}}
{{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legend) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (single-bay structure) | Alternate_names = | Notable_shrines = | Festival = {{ill|April 8|ja|4月8日}} | Rituals = }} '''Omi Shrine''' (乎彌神社, Omi Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. == Deities == * {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}} (Sea God) * {{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} (worshipped at {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}) == Shrine crest == * Left Mitsudomoe (三つ巴) == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. It is identified as the small shrine "Omi Jinja" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Ika District|ja|伊香郡}}, {{ill|Omi Province|ja|近江国}}, and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (disputed location).Other possible locations for the shrine include {{ill|Omi Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|大見神社}} in Kinomoto Town, Nagahama, and {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|春日神社 (長浜市高月町宇根)}} in Takatsuki Town, Nagahama. The shrine also enshrines the deities of the {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}, another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. During the [[Meiji period]], four shrines within the shrine parish—Murakusa Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Daimyojin Shrine, and Seki Shrine—were relocated to the shrine grounds. It was designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Festivals == * Main Festival: April 8 == Auxiliary shrines == * {{ill|Murakusa Shrine|ja|村草神社}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} * {{ill|Daimyojin Shrine|ja|大名持神社}} * {{ill|Seki Shrine|ja|塞神社}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == * revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Omi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:Omi-jinja.JPG|center|250px]]
Front view of Omi Shrine | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shimoyogo 2053 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|56.5|N|136|12|16.3|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}}
{{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}}
{{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legend) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (single-bay structure) | Alternate_names = | Notable_shrines = | Festival = {{ill|April 8|ja|4月8日}} | Rituals = }} '''Omi Shrine''' (乎彌神社, Omi Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Yogo Town, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. == Deities == * {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}} (Sea God) * {{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命}} (worshipped at {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}) == Shrine crest == * Left Mitsudomoe (三つ巴) == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. It is identified as the small shrine "Omi Jinja" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Ika District|ja|伊香郡}}, {{ill|Omi Province|ja|近江国}}, and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (disputed location).Other possible locations for the shrine include {{ill|Omi Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|大見神社}} in Kinomoto Town, Nagahama, and {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|春日神社 (長浜市高月町宇根)}} in Takatsuki Town, Nagahama. The shrine also enshrines the deities of the {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社}}, another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. During the [[Meiji period]], four shrines within the shrine parish—Murakusa Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Daimyojin Shrine, and Seki Shrine—were relocated to the shrine grounds. It was designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Festivals == * Main Festival: April 8 == Auxiliary shrines == * {{ill|Murakusa Shrine|ja|村草神社}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} * {{ill|Daimyojin Shrine|ja|大名持神社}} * {{ill|Seki Shrine|ja|塞神社}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1267 Omi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{ja icon}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Omi Shrine (Yogo)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nagahama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagahama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Omi Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Omi Shrine (Yogo)]] [[ja:乎彌神社]] [[en:Omi Shrine (Yogo)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Omi Shrine (Yogo)}}

Front view of Omi Shrine | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市|12=draft}}, Yogo Town, Shimoyogo 2053 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|56.5|N|136|12|16.3|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命|12=draft}}
{{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命|12=draft}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} (minor) (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}} | Established = (Legend) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}} | Main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} (single-bay structure) | Alternate_names = | Notable_shrines = | Festival = {{ill|April 8|ja|4月8日|12=draft}} | Rituals = }} '''Omi Shrine''' (乎彌神社, Omi Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市|12=draft}}, Yogo Town, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. == Deities == * {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神|12=draft}} (Sea God) * {{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命|12=draft}} (worshipped at {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社|12=draft}}) == Shrine crest == * Left Mitsudomoe (三つ巴) == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}}. It is identified as the small shrine "Omi Jinja" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} for {{ill|Ika District|ja|伊香郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Omi Province|ja|近江国|12=draft}}, and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} (disputed location).Other possible locations for the shrine include {{ill|Omi Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|大見神社|12=draft}} in Kinomoto Town, Nagahama, and {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|春日神社 (長浜市高月町宇根)|12=draft}} in Takatsuki Town, Nagahama. The shrine also enshrines the deities of the {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社|12=draft}}, another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}}. During the [[Meiji period]], four shrines within the shrine parish—Murakusa Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Daimyojin Shrine, and Seki Shrine—were relocated to the shrine grounds. It was designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Festivals == * Main Festival: April 8 == Auxiliary shrines == * {{ill|Murakusa Shrine|ja|村草神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Daimyojin Shrine|ja|大名持神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Seki Shrine|ja|塞神社|12=draft}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1267 Omi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{ja icon}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Omi Shrine (Yogo)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nagahama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagahama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Omi Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Omi Shrine (Yogo)]] [[ja:乎彌神社]] [[en:Omi Shrine (Yogo)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Omi Shrine (Yogo)}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11369800]] Wikidata name: Omi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Omi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Omi-jinja.JPG | location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]], Yogo Town, Shimoyogo 2053 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.532361|N|136.204528|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kochihito-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}}
{{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 梨迹臣命|1=Nashitomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108616765}} | Shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor) (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = (Legend) During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | Main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (single-bay structure) | Alternate_names = | Notable_shrines = | Festival = [[:en:April 8|April 8]] | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Nagahama|qid=Q844130}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Omi Shrine''' (乎彌神社, Omi Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]], Yogo Town, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. == Deities == * {{ill|Kochihito-no-Mikoto|ja|巨知人命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kochihito-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}} (Sea God) * {{ill|Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨津臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasitomi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 梨迹臣命|1=Nashitomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108616765}} (worshipped at {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noimi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 乎彌神社|1=Omi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369800}}) == Shrine crest == * Left Mitsudomoe (三つ巴) == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. It is identified as the small shrine "Omi Jinja" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for [[:en:Ika District, Shiga|Ika District]], [[:en:Ōmi Province|Omi Province]], and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (disputed location).Other possible locations for the shrine include {{ill|Omi Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|大見神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Shrine (Nagahama)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kinomoto Town, Nagahama, and {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Nagahama)|ja|春日神社 (長浜市高月町宇根)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Nagahama)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Takatsuki Town, Nagahama. The shrine also enshrines the deities of the {{ill|Noimi Shrine|ja|乃弥神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noimi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 乎彌神社|1=Omi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369800}}, another {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], four shrines within the shrine parish—Murakusa Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Daimyojin Shrine, and Seki Shrine—were relocated to the shrine grounds. It was designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system. == Festivals == * Main Festival: April 8 == Auxiliary shrines == * {{ill|Murakusa Shrine|ja|村草神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakusa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} * {{ill|Daimyojin Shrine|ja|大名持神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimyojin Shrine|qq=|1=Ōnamochi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594663}} * {{ill|Seki Shrine|ja|塞神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seki Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1267 Omi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{ja icon}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|乎彌神社|version=93048962|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Omi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] }} [[image:Omi-jinja.JPG|center|250px]] [[sn:Omi Shrine (Yogo)]] [[ja:乎彌神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagahama, Shiga]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]
